title: Keeping the viewer up to date
question: How does the viewer page stay current without re-running the build script by hand after every change?
rationale: Rebuild the viewer automatically — on every add/revise and at end-of-session when files changed — so you never run the build by hand. Kept over the live-read local-server option to preserve the double-click, offline, no-server property (d1, d2).

options.label: Rebuild automatically (on add/revise, and end of session)

tradeoffs.note: logging a decision rebuilds the page; no script to run
stays a double-click file; no server needed
the rebuild happens quietly, not as a visible step

options.label: Re-run the build by hand (leave as is)

tradeoffs.note: re-run the script after every edit

options.label: Load the data live via a local server

tradeoffs.note: edit the data, refresh the browser, no rebuild
a browser security rule blocks this unless a local server is running

options.label: Drag the data file onto the page

tradeoffs.note: no script, but you pick the file each time you open it
